title: Privacy audit
subtitle: See what your own tracks reveal — home, routine, regularity — then mask it before you share.
rationale: GPS tracks de-anonymize easily: clustered start points reveal where you live, and weekday/time regularity reveals your routine. This is a defensive self-audit of your own data, run locally — the inverse of a public heatmap. It recommends a privacy radius that feeds the anonymization the app already ships. Nothing is transmitted, and the inferred location is never stored.

sources.title: Fitness tracking app Strava gives away location of secret US army bases (2018 global heatmap)
Updates to Strava's API Agreement (users 'unaware their data is surfaced in a public feed or heatmap')
Strava's new API agreement will destroy the app, users warn
